member.name: T:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.ArrayPoolBufferWriter`1
member.summary: Pooled Buffer Writer

member.summary: PooledBufferWriter constructor

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.ArrayPoolBufferWriter`1.#ctor(System.Int32)
member.summary: Constructs a PooledBufferWriter
member.param: The size of the initial buffer

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.ArrayPoolBufferWriter`1.Advance(System.Int32)
member.summary: Advanced the current position

member.summary: Writes the provided value

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.ArrayPoolBufferWriter`1.Write(System.ReadOnlySpan{`0})
member.summary: Writes the provided values

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.ArrayPoolBufferWriter`1.GetMemory(System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns a writeable block of memory that can be written to

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.ArrayPoolBufferWriter`1.GetSpan(System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns a span that can be written to

member.name: P:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.ArrayPoolBufferWriter`1.WrittenCount
member.summary: Returns the number of bytes written to the buffer

member.name: P:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.ArrayPoolBufferWriter`1.WrittenMemory
member.summary: Returns the committed data as Memory

member.name: P:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.ArrayPoolBufferWriter`1.WrittenSpan
member.summary: Returns the committed data as a Span

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.ArrayPoolBufferWriter`1.Reset(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Resets the internal state so the instance can be reused before disposal

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.ArrayPoolBufferWriter`1.Dispose
member.summary: Disposes the buffer and returns any rented memory to the pool

member.name: T:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.FillingRule
member.summary: Rules for determining which points lie inside/outside a path.

member.name: F:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.FillingRule.None
member.summary: No rule.

member.name: F:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.FillingRule.EvenOdd
member.summary: This even-odd rule determines whether a point is inside a path by drawing a ray from that point in any direction and simply counting the number of path segments that cross the ray, regardless of direction. If this number is odd, the point is inside; if even, the point is outside. This yields the same results as the nonzero winding number rule for paths with simple shapes, but produces different results for more complex shapes.

member.name: F:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.FillingRule.NonZeroWinding
member.summary: The nonzero winding number rule determines whether a given point is inside a path by conceptually drawing a ray from that point to infinity in any direction and then examining the places where a segment of the path crosses the ray. Starting with a count of 0, the rule adds 1 each time a path segment crosses the ray from left to right and subtracts 1 each time a segment crosses from right to left. After counting all the crossings, if the result is 0, the point is outside the path; otherwise, it is inside.

member.name: T:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.IDeepCloneable`1
member.summary: Indicates the type may be cloned making entirely independent copies.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.IDeepCloneable`1.DeepClone
member.summary: Clone the type including all referenced data.

member.name: T:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.IInputBytes
member.summary: The input bytes for a PDF document.

member.name: P:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.IInputBytes.CurrentOffset
member.summary: The current offset in bytes.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.IInputBytes.MoveNext
member.summary: Moves to the next byte if available.

member.name: P:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.IInputBytes.CurrentByte
member.summary: The current byte.

member.name: P:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.IInputBytes.Length
member.summary: The length of the data in bytes.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.IInputBytes.Peek
member.summary: Returns the next byte if available.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.IInputBytes.IsAtEnd
member.summary: Whether we are at the end of the available data.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.IInputBytes.Seek(System.Int64)
member.summary: Move to a given position.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.IInputBytes.Read(System.Span{System.Byte})
member.summary: Fill the buffer with bytes starting from the current position.
member.param: A buffer with a length corresponding to the number of bytes to read.
member.returns: The number of bytes successfully read.

member.name: T:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.IndirectReference
member.summary: Used to uniquely identify and refer to objects in the PDF file.

member.name: P:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.IndirectReference.ObjectNumber
member.summary: A positive integer object number.

member.name: P:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.IndirectReference.Generation
member.summary:
A non-negative integer generation number which starts as 0 and increases if the file is updated incrementally.
The maximum generation number is 65,535.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.IndirectReference.#ctor(System.Int64,System.Int32)
member.summary: Create a new
member.param: The object number.
The generation number.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.IndirectReference.ComputeInternalHash(System.Int64,System.Int32)
member.summary: Calculate the internal hash value for the given object number and generation number.
member.param: The object number.
The generation number.
member.returns: The internal hash for the given values.

member.summary: Indicates that a data structure can be written to an output stream.

member.summary: Write the data to the output stream.

member.name: T:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.OtherEncodings
member.summary: Convenience access to frequently used encodings.

member.name: F:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.OtherEncodings.Iso88591
member.summary: Latin 1 Encoding: ISO 8859-1 is a single-byte encoding that can represent the first 256 Unicode characters.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.OtherEncodings.StringAsLatin1Bytes(System.String)
member.summary: Convert the string to bytes using the ISO 8859-1 encoding.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.OtherEncodings.BytesAsLatin1String(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Byte})
member.summary: Convert the bytes to string using the ISO 8859-1 encoding.

summary.para: PDFDocEncoding, defined in the spec for text strings in PDF objects (but not content stream contents, Type 1 font contents, etc).

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfDocEncoding.TryConvertBytesToString(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Byte},System.String@)
member.summary: Try to convert raw bytes to a PdfDocEncoding encoded string. If unsupported characters are encountered meaning we cannot safely round-trip the value to bytes this will instead return false.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfDocEncoding.StringToBytes(System.String)
member.summary: Map from string back to bytes. This is not a reversible operation for all inputs.

member.name: T:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfDocumentFormatException
member.summary: This exception will be thrown where the contents of the PDF document do not match the specification in such a way that it renders the document unreadable.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfDocumentFormatException.#ctor
member.summary: Create a new .

member.name: T:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfLine
member.summary: A line in a PDF file.
member.remarks: PDF coordinates are defined with the origin at the lower left (0, 0). The Y-axis extends vertically upwards and the X-axis horizontally to the right. Unless otherwise specified on a per-page basis, units in PDF space are equivalent to a typographic point (1/72 inch).

member.name: P:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfLine.Length
member.summary: Length of the line.

member.name: P:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfLine.Point1
member.summary: First point of the line.

member.name: P:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfLine.Point2
member.summary: Second point of the line.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfLine.#ctor(System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: Create a new .
member.param: The x coordinate of the first point on the line.
The y coordinate of the first point on the line.
The x coordinate of the second point on the line.
The y coordinate of the second point on the line.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfLine.#ctor(UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfPoint,UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfPoint)
member.summary: Create a new .
member.param: First point of the line.
Second point of the line.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfLine.GetBoundingRectangle
member.summary: The rectangle completely containing the .

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfLine.Equals(System.Object)
member.summary: Returns a value indicating whether this is equal to a specified .

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfLine.Equals(UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfLine)
member.summary: Returns a value indicating whether this is equal to a specified .

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfLine.GetHashCode
member.summary: Returns the hash code for this .

member.name: T:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfPoint
member.summary: A point in a PDF file.
member.remarks: PDF coordinates are defined with the origin at the lower left (0, 0). The Y-axis extends vertically upwards and the X-axis horizontally to the right. Unless otherwise specified on a per-page basis, units in PDF space are equivalent to a typographic point (1/72 inch).

member.name: P:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfPoint.Origin
member.summary: The origin of the coordinates system.

member.name: P:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfPoint.X
member.summary: The X coordinate for this point. (Horizontal axis).

member.name: P:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfPoint.Y
member.summary: The Y coordinate of this point. (Vertical axis).

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfPoint.#ctor(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Create a new at this position.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfPoint.#ctor(System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: Create a new at this position.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfPoint.MoveX(System.Double)
member.summary: Creates a new which is the current point moved in the x direction relative to its current position by a value.
member.param: The distance to move the point in the x direction relative to its current location.
member.returns: A new point shifted on the x axis by the given delta value.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfPoint.MoveY(System.Double)
member.summary: Creates a new which is the current point moved in the y direction relative to its current position by a value.
member.param: The distance to move the point in the y direction relative to its current location.
member.returns: A new point shifted on the y axis by the given delta value.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfPoint.Translate(System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: Creates a new which is the current point moved in the x and y directions relative to its current position by a value.
member.param: The distance to move the point in the x direction relative to its current location.
The distance to move the point in the y direction relative to its current location.
member.returns: A new point shifted on the y axis by the given delta value.

member.summary: Returns a value indicating whether this is equal to a specified .

member.summary: Returns a value indicating whether this is equal to a specified .

member.summary: Returns the hash code for this .

member.name: T:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfRange
member.summary: This class will be used to signify a range. a(min) <= a* <= a(max)

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfRange.#ctor(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.Double})
member.summary: Constructor assumes a starting index of 0.
member.param: The array that describes the range.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfRange.#ctor(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.Double},System.Int32)
member.summary: Constructor with an index into an array. Because some arrays specify multiple ranges ie [0, 1, 0, 2, 2, 3]. It is convenient for this class to take an index into an array. So if you want this range to represent 0, 2 in the above example then you would say new PDRange(array, 1).
member.param: The array that describes the index
The range index into the array for the start of the range.

member.name: P:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfRange.Min
member.summary: The minimum value of the range.

member.name: P:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfRange.Max
member.summary: The maximum value of the range.

member.name: T:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfRectangle
member.summary: A rectangle in a PDF file.
member.remarks: PDF coordinates are defined with the origin at the lower left (0, 0). The Y-axis extends vertically upwards and the X-axis horizontally to the right. Unless otherwise specified on a per-page basis, units in PDF space are equivalent to a typographic point (1/72 inch).

member.summary: Centroid point of the rectangle.

member.summary:
Width of the rectangle.
A positive number.

member.summary:
Height of the rectangle.
A positive number.

member.name: P:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfRectangle.Rotation
member.summary:
Rotation angle of the rectangle. Counterclockwise, in degrees.
-180 ≤ θ ≤ 180

member.name: P:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfRectangle.Area
member.summary: Area of the rectangle.

member.summary: Left. This value is only valid if the rectangle is not rotated, check .

member.summary: Top. This value is only valid if the rectangle is not rotated, check .

member.summary: Bottom. This value is only valid if the rectangle is not rotated, check .

member.summary: Create a new .
member.param: Bottom left point of the rectangle.
Top right point of the rectangle.

member.summary: Create a new .
member.param: Bottom left point's x coordinate of the rectangle.
Bottom left point's y coordinate of the rectangle.
Top right point's x coordinate of the rectangle.
Top right point's y coordinate of the rectangle.

member.summary: Create a new .

member.summary: Create a new .

member.summary: Creates a new which is the current rectangle moved in the x and y directions relative to its current position by a value.

member.name: T:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfSubpath
member.summary: A supbpath is made up of a sequence of connected segments.

member.name: P:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfSubpath.Commands
member.summary: The sequence of commands which form this .

member.name: P:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfSubpath.IsDrawnAsRectangle
member.summary:
True if the was originaly drawn using the rectangle ('re') operator.
Always false if paths are clipped.

member.name: P:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfSubpath.IsClockwise
member.summary: Return true if points are organised in a clockwise order. Works only with closed paths.

member.name: P:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfSubpath.IsCounterClockwise
member.summary: Return true if points are organised in a counterclockwise order. Works only with closed paths.

member.summary: Get the 's centroid point.

member.summary: Simplify this by converting everything to s.
member.param: Number of lines required (minimum is 1).

member.summary: Add a command to the path.

member.summary: Add a command to the path.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfSubpath.Rectangle(System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: Add a rectangle following the pdf specification (m, l, l, l, c) path. A new subpath is created.

member.summary: Add a to the path.

member.summary:
Add a to the path.
Only used in fonts.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfSubpath.CloseSubpath
member.summary: Close the path.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfSubpath.IsClosed
member.summary: Determines if the path is currently closed.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfSubpath.GetBoundingRectangle
member.summary: Gets a which entirely contains the geometry of the defined subpath.
member.returns: For subpaths which don't define any geometry this returns .

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfSubpath.GetDrawnRectangle
member.summary: If then returns the rectangle dimensions specified. Otherwise returns .
member.remarks: Since a rectangle is interpreted as a move command followed by 3 lines and a close command this condenses the 5 commands back into a single rectangle.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfSubpath.GetBoundingRectangle(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfSubpath})
member.summary: Gets a which entirely contains the geometry of the defined path.
member.returns: For paths which don't define any geometry this returns .

member.summary: A command in a .

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfSubpath.IPathCommand.GetBoundingRectangle
member.summary: Returns the smallest rectangle which contains the path region given by this command.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfSubpath.IPathCommand.WriteSvg(System.Text.StringBuilder,System.Double)
member.summary: Converts from the path command to an SVG string representing the path operation.

member.name: T:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfSubpath.Close
member.summary: Close the current .

Draw a quadratic Bezier-curve given by the start, control and end points.
Only used in fonts.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfSubpath.BezierCurve.ValueWithT(System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: Calculate the value of the Quadratic Bezier-curve at t.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfSubpath.BezierCurve.ValueWithT(System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: Calculate the value of the Cubic Bezier-curve at t.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfSubpath.BezierCurve.ToLines(System.Int32)
member.summary: Converts the bezier curve into approximated lines.
member.param: Number of lines required (minimum is 1).

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfSubpath.Equals(System.Object)
member.summary: Compares two s for equality. Paths will only be considered equal if the commands which construct the paths are in the same order.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfSubpath.GetHashCode
member.summary: Get the hash code. Paths will only have the same hash code if the commands which construct the paths are in the same order.

member.name: T:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.ReadHelper
member.summary: Helper methods for reading from PDF files.

member.name: F:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.ReadHelper.AsciiLineFeed
member.summary: The line-feed '\n' character.

member.name: F:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.ReadHelper.AsciiCarriageReturn
member.summary: The carriage return '\r' character.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.ReadHelper.ReadLine(UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.IInputBytes)
member.summary: Read a string from the input until a newline.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.ReadHelper.SkipSpaces(UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.IInputBytes)
member.summary: Skip any whitespace characters.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.ReadHelper.IsEndOfName(System.Int32)
member.summary: Whether the given character value is the end of a PDF Name token.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.ReadHelper.IsWhitespace(System.Byte)
member.summary: Determines if a character is whitespace or not, this includes newlines.
member.remarks: These values are specified in table 1 (page 12) of ISO 32000-1:2008.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.ReadHelper.IsEndOfLine(System.Char)
member.summary: Whether the character is an end of line character.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.ReadHelper.IsEndOfLine(System.Byte)
member.summary: Whether the character is an end of line character.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.ReadHelper.IsLineFeed(System.Nullable{System.Byte})
member.summary: Whether the character is an line feed '\n' character.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.ReadHelper.IsCarriageReturn(System.Byte)
member.summary: Whether the character is a carriage return '\r' character.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.ReadHelper.IsString(UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.IInputBytes,System.String)
member.summary: Whether the given string is at this position in the input. Resets to the current offset once read.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.ReadHelper.IsString(UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.IInputBytes,System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Byte})
member.summary: Whether the given string is at this position in the input. Resets to the current offset once read.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.ReadHelper.ReadLong(UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.IInputBytes)
member.summary: Read a long from the input.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.ReadHelper.IsDigit(System.Int32)
member.summary: Whether the given value is a digit or not.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.ReadHelper.ReadInt(UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.IInputBytes)
member.summary: Read an int from the input.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.ReadHelper.IsHex(System.Byte)
member.summary: Whether the given character value is a valid hex value.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.ReadHelper.IsHex(System.Char)
member.summary: Whether the given character value is a valid hex value.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.ReadHelper.IsValidUtf8(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Whether the given input bytes are valid UTF8.

member.name: T:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.StackDepthGuard
member.summary: Provides a guard for tracking and limiting the depth of nested stack operations, such as recursive calls or nested parsing.
member.remarks: Use this class to prevent excessive stack usage by enforcing a maximum nesting depth. This is particularly useful in scenarios where untrusted or deeply nested input could cause stack overflows or performance issues.

member.name: F:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.StackDepthGuard.Infinite
member.summary: Represents a stack depth guard with no effective limit on the allowed depth.
member.remarks: Use this instance when stack depth restrictions are not required.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.StackDepthGuard.#ctor(System.Int32)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the StackDepthGuard class with the specified maximum stack depth.
member.param: The maximum allowed stack depth for guarded operations. Must be a positive integer.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.StackDepthGuard.Enter
member.summary: Increments the current nesting depth and checks against the maximum allowed stack depth.
member.exception: Thrown if the maximum allowed nesting depth is exceeded.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.StackDepthGuard.Exit
member.summary: Decreases the current depth level by one, ensuring that the depth does not become negative.
member.remarks: If the current depth is already zero, calling this method has no effect. This method is typically used to track or manage nested operations or scopes where depth must remain non-negative.

member.name: T:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.StreamInputBytes
member.summary: Input bytes from a stream.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.StreamInputBytes.#ctor(System.IO.Stream,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Create a new .
member.param: The stream to use, should be readable and seekable.
Whether this class should dispose the stream once finished.

member.name: T:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.TextRenderingMode
member.summary: The text rendering mode determines whether showing text causes glyph outlines to be stroked, filled, used as a clipping boundary, or some combination of the three.

member.name: F:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.TextRenderingMode.Fill
member.summary: Fill text.
member.remarks: Corresponds to filling the entire letter region.

member.name: F:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.TextRenderingMode.Stroke
member.summary: Stroke text.
member.remarks: Corresponds to drawing the border/outline of the letter.

member.name: F:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.TextRenderingMode.FillThenStroke
member.summary: Fill then stroke text.

member.name: F:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.TextRenderingMode.Neither
member.summary: Neither fill nor stroke text thereby causing text to become invisible.

member.name: F:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.TextRenderingMode.FillClip
member.summary: Fill the text and add to the clipping path.

member.name: F:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.TextRenderingMode.StrokeClip
member.summary: Stroke the text and add to the clipping path.

member.name: F:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.TextRenderingMode.FillThenStrokeClip
member.summary: Fill then stroke the text and then add to the clipping path.

member.name: F:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.TextRenderingMode.NeitherClip
member.summary: Neither fill nor stroke but add to the clipping path.

member.name: T:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.TransformationMatrix
member.summary: Specifies the conversion from the transformed coordinate space to the original untransformed coordinate space.

member.name: F:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.TransformationMatrix.Identity
member.summary: The default .

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.TransformationMatrix.GetTranslationMatrix(System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: Create a new with the X and Y translation values set.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.TransformationMatrix.GetScaleMatrix(System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: Create a new with the X and Y scaling values set.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.TransformationMatrix.GetRotationMatrix(System.Double)
member.summary: Create a new with the X and Y scaling values set.

member.name: F:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.TransformationMatrix.A
member.summary: The value at (0, 0) - The scale for the X dimension.

member.name: F:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.TransformationMatrix.B
member.summary: The value at (0, 1).

member.name: F:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.TransformationMatrix.C
member.summary: The value at (1, 0).

member.name: F:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.TransformationMatrix.D
member.summary: The value at (1, 1) - The scale for the Y dimension.

member.name: F:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.TransformationMatrix.E
member.summary: The value at (2, 0) - translation in X.

member.name: F:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.TransformationMatrix.F
member.summary: The value at (2, 1) - translation in Y.

member.name: P:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.TransformationMatrix.Item(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Get the value at the specific row and column.

member.name: F:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.TransformationMatrix.Rows
member.summary: The number of rows in the matrix.

member.name: F:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.TransformationMatrix.Columns
member.summary: The number of columns in the matrix.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.TransformationMatrix.#ctor(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Double})
member.summary: Create a new .
member.param: The 9 values of the matrix.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.TransformationMatrix.#ctor(System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: Create a new .

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.TransformationMatrix.Transform(UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfPoint)
member.summary: Transform a point using this transformation matrix.
member.param: The original point.
member.returns: A new point which is the result of applying this transformation matrix.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.TransformationMatrix.Transform(System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: Transform a point using this transformation matrix.
member.param: The original point X coordinate.
The original point Y coordinate.
member.returns: A new point which is the result of applying this transformation matrix.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.TransformationMatrix.TransformX(System.Double)
member.summary: Transform an X coordinate using this transformation matrix.
member.param: The X coordinate.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.TransformationMatrix.TransformY(System.Double)
member.summary: Transform an Y coordinate using this transformation matrix.
member.param: The Y coordinate.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.TransformationMatrix.Transform(UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfRectangle)
member.summary: Transform a rectangle using this transformation matrix.
member.param: The original rectangle.
member.returns: A new rectangle which is the result of applying this transformation matrix.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.TransformationMatrix.Transform(UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfSubpath)
member.summary: Transform a subpath using this transformation matrix.
member.param: The original subpath.
member.returns: A new subpath which is the result of applying this transformation matrix.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.TransformationMatrix.Transform(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.PdfSubpath})
member.summary: Transform a path using this transformation matrix.
member.param: The original path.
member.returns: A new path which is the result of applying this transformation matrix.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.TransformationMatrix.Translate(System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: Generate a translated by the specified amount.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.TransformationMatrix.FromValues(System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: Create a new from the 6 values provided in the default PDF order.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.TransformationMatrix.FromValues(System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: Create a new from the 4 values provided in the default PDF order.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.TransformationMatrix.FromArray(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Double})
member.summary: Create a new from the values.
member.param: Either all 9 values of the matrix, 6 values in the default PDF order or the 4 values of the top left square.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.TransformationMatrix.Multiply(UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.TransformationMatrix@)
member.summary: Multiplies one transformation matrix by another without modifying either matrix. Order is: (this * matrix).
member.param: The matrix to multiply

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.TransformationMatrix.Multiply(System.Double)
member.summary: Multiplies the matrix by a scalar value without modifying this matrix.
member.param: The value to multiply.
member.returns: A new matrix which is multiplied by the scalar value.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.TransformationMatrix.Inverse
member.summary: Get the inverse of the current matrix.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.TransformationMatrix.GetScalingFactorX
member.summary: Get the X scaling component of the current matrix.
member.returns: The scaling factor for the x dimension in this matrix.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.TransformationMatrix.Equals(UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.TransformationMatrix,UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.TransformationMatrix)
member.summary: Determines whether 2 transformation matrices are equal.

member.name: T:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.Union`2
member.summary: Defines a type which is a union of two types.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.Union`2.Match(System.Action{`0},System.Action{`1})
member.summary: Take an action for the item of the given type.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.Union`2.Match``1(System.Func{`0,``0},System.Func{`1,``0})
member.summary: Run a func against the item of the given type.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.Union`2.TryGetFirst(`0@)
member.summary: Get the item if it is of the specific type.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.Union`2.TryGetSecond(`1@)
member.summary: Get the item if it is of the specific type.

member.summary: Create a value of the first type.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.Union`2.Two(`1)
member.summary: Create a value of the second type.

member.summary: The type representing items of the first type in a union.

member.summary: The item.

member.summary: The type representing items of the second type in a union.

member.name: F:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.Union`2.Case2.Item
member.summary: The item.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.Union`2.Case2.#ctor(`1)
member.summary: Create second type.

member.name: T:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.WritingExtensions
member.summary: Handles writing specified data types to an output stream in a valid PDF compliant format.

member.summary: Write the to the stream as a .

member.name: T:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.XrefEntryType
member.summary: Indicates where an object is located in the Xref.

member.name: F:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.XrefEntryType.Free
member.summary: Free object.

member.name: F:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.XrefEntryType.File
member.summary: Located as an object in the file.

member.name: F:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.XrefEntryType.ObjectStream
member.summary: Located in a compressed object stream.

member.name: T:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.XrefLocation
member.summary: Information about where an object is located in the file according to the Xref (or brute force parsing).

member.name: F:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.XrefLocation.Type
member.summary: Which type of location is indicated.

member.name: F:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.XrefLocation.Value1
member.summary: If is then byte offset, otherwise this is the stream number.

member.name: F:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.XrefLocation.Value2
member.summary: If is then the index of the object in the stream.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.XrefLocation.File(System.Int64)
member.summary: Create a location mapped to a byte offset in the file.

member.name: M:UglyToad.PdfPig.Core.XrefLocation.Stream(System.Int64,System.Int32)
member.summary: Create a location mapped to an index inside and object stream.
